title: Is Innodata's New Palantir Partnership a Long-Term Catalyst?
text: Innodata Inc. has partnered with Palantir Technologies to provide data engineering and training data services. The collaboration involves annotating thousands of hours of rodeo video footage to develop computer vision models that identify animals, riders, and skeletal movements for performance analytics in bull riding and barrel racing. Following the announcement, INOD stock rose 14.4% in trading. Key details include: Innodata expands its role in physical AI workflows by delivering mission-critical training data and engineering services. The partnership positions Innodata as a specialized data partner handling complex datasets across video, imagery, documents, and sensor feeds. The deal reflects growing enterprise demand for sophisticated data engineering as AI becomes central to competitive strategy. Valuation context shows: INOD trades at a forward P/E of 53.07, significantly above the industry average of 24.99. The stock has risen 72.2% over the past year. Zacks Consensus Estimate for 2026 earnings remains at $1.20. The stock carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) rating.
